stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (horizontal scaling) yields a guaranteed return of $89K. Option B (vertical scaling) depends on traffic load patterns: with probability 33-51% (uncertain), it yields $255K; otherwise it yields $29K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic load patterns affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $89K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $103.6K to $144.3K. Point estimate: $123.9K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 26.5%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 26.5%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
